steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the two clauses
steps.content: The first clause states that the Center has cultivated a multigenerational community of Black creatives. The second clause states that it has garnered a reputation as a hub of Black art and culture. The second event is a result of the first, so the transition must signal consequence.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'accordingly,': Accordingly means "as a result," correctly marking the second clause as a consequence of the first. ✓
  • Choice B — 'on the other hand,': On the other hand signals contrast; there is no contrast between cultivating creatives and gaining a reputation. ✗
  • Choice C — 'nevertheless,': Nevertheless signals concession or contradiction; the second clause is not surprising given the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'periodically,': Periodically is a time adverb meaning "at intervals"; it does not express any logical relationship between the clauses. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
